notes: Lesson 2 Part 2 (act 11, "Sensitivity and specificity run the test", 298.18s, 14 cues rescaled to real MP3). New reusable idioms added to manim/bayes.py: confusion_matrix() (2x2 TP/FP/FN/TN with row/col headers + optional counts), sensitivity_formula(), specificity_formula(). Arc: two dials -> the confusion matrix sorts every outcome -> detective-board analogy -> concrete 100-person counts (TP1/FP5/FN0/TN94) -> sensitivity catches sick (sick column) -> specificity blocks healthy (healthy column) -> false-alarm rate P(+|healthy)=5% -> positive row = TP+FP = 6 -> the base-rate mistake P(+|sick)!=P(sick|+) -> posterior 1/6~=17%. Two review iterations: round-1 authored+rendered all 14; round-2 fixed Cue09 (side note crowding the matrix -> moved to bottom caption) and Cue10 ("positive row" label colliding with the column headers -> lifted above headers, matrix lowered) plus two pre-render safe-area repositions (Cue09 matrix left edge, Cue05 right-side matrix scale). Per-cue checklist (formula legibility+correctness, highlighted element matches narration, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping, chunk timing): all pass. Timing verified in render log: every cue within pinning tolerance, final 298.28s vs audio 298.18s d0.10s (<0.5s). Concrete model shared with Part 1: 100 people, prior 1%, sensitivity 99%, specificity 95%.

BEFORE: 'the test is 99% accurate' — a number with no base rate attached
start frame: reveal in progress, expected sparse
AFTER: P(sick|+)=TP/(TP+FP)=1/(1+5)=1/6~=17%, a usable answer
